after.task: Finish dashboard status support for changes-requested and repair-needed lanes
after.nextAction: Approved. Changes are well-scoped to serve-dashboard.js only. HOT_SEAT_RESOLVERS correctly routes changes-requested to writer and repair-needed to repairOwner (falling back to writer). STATUS_CLASS_NAMES replaces the old if-chain with a clean lookup. Lane regex widened from single-char to multi-char ids. Repair Owner parsed from handoff file and surfaced as FIX // label in card rendering. CSS variables and animations added for both new states. needs-review color toned down from #ff2a2a to #aa0000 which is a reasonable visual distinction. node -c passes; live smoke test gap is acceptable given the blocked port.

details.summary: Approved. Changes are well-scoped to serve-dashboard.js only. HOT_SEAT_RESOLVERS correctly routes changes-requested to writer and repair-needed to repairOwner (falling back to writer). STATUS_CLASS_NAMES replaces the old if-chain with a clean lookup. Lane regex widened from single-char to multi-char ids. Repair Owner parsed from handoff file and surfaced as FIX // label in card rendering. CSS variables and animations added for both new states. needs-review color toned down from #ff2a2a to #aa0000 which is a reasonable visual distinction. node -c passes; live smoke test gap is acceptable given the blocked port.

after.task: Run live dashboard smoke validation for repair-needed and changes-requested rendering
after.nextAction: Pre-flight review confirmed a browser-only regression in the dashboard client script: renderStatusClass referenced a server-scope constant, so the page never rendered the new statuses even though /api/status was correct. Fix is in progress alongside a focused regression test.

after.task: Run live dashboard smoke validation for repair-needed and changes-requested rendering
after.nextAction: Approved. All 4 e2e tests pass including the new dashboard rendering test. Hazard-tape CSS for repair-needed reads well — repeating-linear-gradient diagonal stripes with white text-stroke for contrast. The e2e test is thorough: sets up two lanes (changes-requested + repair-needed), starts the dashboard on an ephemeral port, validates both /api/status JSON payload (status, hotSeat, repairOwner) and the HTML output (embedded statusClassNames, repair-needed-tape token, client-side renderStatusClass). PORT override via env is low-risk — defaults to 3333 for normal usage. The client-side status map is correctly embedded via JSON.stringify from the server-side constant.

details.summary: Approved. All 4 e2e tests pass including the new dashboard rendering test. Hazard-tape CSS for repair-needed reads well — repeating-linear-gradient diagonal stripes with white text-stroke for contrast. The e2e test is thorough: sets up two lanes (changes-requested + repair-needed), starts the dashboard on an ephemeral port, validates both /api/status JSON payload (status, hotSeat, repairOwner) and the HTML output (embedded statusClassNames, repair-needed-tape token, client-side renderStatusClass). PORT override via env is low-risk — defaults to 3333 for normal usage. The client-side status map is correctly embedded via JSON.stringify from the server-side constant.
